items.title: RCMP visit Edmonton offices of business at centre of Alberta health procurement probes
items.summary_auto: RCMP officers and vehicles were seen on Thursday at the west Edmonton offices of a businessman at the centre of multiple probes into the Alberta government's procurement practices.

items.title: U.S. man with 'good intentions' sentenced for human smuggling across Manitoba border
items.summary_auto: A Manitoba judge has handed a U.S. citizen a nearly three-year prison sentence after he admitted to smuggling a man into Canada near the Emerson border crossing.

items.title: ABC cancels Bachelorette season with Taylor Frankie Paul, citing 2023 video
items.summary_auto: ABC's cancellation of the already taped season of the reality TV show comes days before it was to premiere on Sunday. A Disney Entertainment Television statement appeared to refer to a 2023 video of an altercation between Paul and Dakota Mortensen that was published by TMZ on Thursday.

items.title: Amid fuel crunch, Cuba mechanic converts car to run on charcoal
items.summary_auto: A Cuban mechanic has converted his 1980 Fiat Polski to run on charcoal, a cheaper and more abundant fuel than gasoline since Washington cut off oil shipments to the Caribbean island in January.

items.title: Hamilton set to have AHL team again as New York Islanders plan affiliate's move from Bridgeport, Conn.
items.summary_auto: Hamilton is set to be home to an American Hockey League team again. The NHL's New York Islanders plan to move their feeder team in Bridgeport, Conn., to the Ontario city, pending approval by the AHL.

items.title: Trump to Japan's PM: 'Why didn’t you tell me about Pearl Harbor?'
items.summary_auto: When asked why the U.S. didn't consult allies in Asia and Europe before attacking Iran, U.S. President Donald Trump said his administration wanted the attack to be a 'surprise,' and made a joke referencing Japan's military strike on Pearl Harbor.

items.title: U.S. congressman targets Canada's 'Netflix tax' with new bill
items.summary_auto: A new Republican-sponsored bill in the U.S. Congress takes aim at Canada's attempts to force foreign streaming companies such as Netflix and Disney+ to pay into Canadian funds for domestic content production.

items.title: New trial ordered for former Quebec junior hockey player in sexual assault case
items.summary_auto: Quebec's Court of Appeal overturned Noah Corson's guilty verdict. He was orginally sentenced to two years less a day in prison for sexual assault of a minor under the age of 16.

items.title: Brandon Tobin killed his grandmother after a drug-induced seizure. A judge will soon decide his fate
items.summary_auto: Crown prosecutors are asking for a six-year prison sentence while the defence has countered with three to four, arguing Brandon Tobin didn't choose to kill his grandmother — he chose to use drugs, which led to her death.

items.title: The facts about floor-crossing in Canada
items.summary_auto: Politicians switching parties is rare, but Canada has a long history of it. Here are the facts about crossing the floor.

items.title: Aamjiwnaang First Nation feels left in the dark after Suncor oil spill in St. Clair River in Sarnia, Ont.
items.summary_auto: The First Nation wants to know how much was spilled, what caused it and the remediation plans

items.title: Netflix Canada to adapt Carley Fortune's This Summer Will Be Different into new TV series
items.summary_auto: This Summer Will Be Different is set on Prince Edward Island and in Toronto.

items.title: 19 Kids and Counting star Joseph Duggar arrested, accused of child sexual abuse
items.summary_auto: Joseph Duggar, former star of the TLC show 19 Kids and Counting, was arrested and charged in Arkansas Wednesday for lewd and lascivious behaviour involving unlawful sexual activity with a minor, authorities said.

items.title: Canada's top court will hear challenges to Liberals' firearms ban
items.summary_auto: The Supreme Court will hear arguments against the Liberal government's controversial decision to outlaw certain firearms.

items.title: Can Lebanon's Hezbollah survive another war with Israel?
items.summary_auto: With its ally Iran now at war with the U.S. and Israel, and the Israel Defence Forces pounding Hezbollah’s weapons storage and infrastructure and killing senior leadership, can the militant group survive another war?

items.title: 2 men deported over alleged roles in extortions targeting Edmonton's South Asian community, police say
items.summary_auto: Edmonton police along with federal law enforcement deported two men for their connection to extortion schemes targeting business owners in the city's South Asian community. Similar criminal schemes have seen businesses burned across Edmonton in recent years.

items.title: Canada fast-tracks army rifle upgrade with $307M initial order
items.summary_auto: Canada is fast-tracking the purchase of more than 65,000 new assault rifles, starting with a $307 million order for 30,000 weapons. Built by Colt Canada, the rifles will replace aging C7 and C8 models, marking a push to modernize the army and speed up procurement through a streamlined process.

items.title: Coquitlam mudslide knocks out power as heaviest rainfall expected on parts of B.C.'s South Coast
items.summary_auto: Environment Canada says the heaviest rainfall from the "prolonged atmospheric river event" in parts of Metro Vancouver, the Sea-to-Sky region and western Vancouver Island is expected Thursday night into Friday morning.

items.title: Kevin Spacey settles sex assault claims out of court ahead of U.K. civil trial
items.summary_auto: The award-winning actor settled civil cases with three men who alleged Spacey sexually abused them at times between 2000 and 2013. Spacey denied the accusations.

items.title: Afroman wins lawsuit with Ohio police who said rapper mocked them in viral music video
items.summary_auto: "We did it, America! ... Freedom of speech!" the rapper shouted outside the courthouse, dressed in a red, white and blue American flag suit. Seven Ohio sheriffs deputies sued Afroman for using home security footage to mock their 2022 raid of his home.

items.title: North Korea qualifies for the Women's World Cup for the first time since 2011
items.summary_auto: North Korea has qualified for the Women's World Cup for the first time since a doping saga in 2011 led to its long absence from top-tier international tournaments.

items.title: Yukon premier tables $82M deficit budget, blames previous government's spending
items.summary_auto: When Yukon Premier Currie Dixon tabled his government’s first full budget on Thursday, he put it bluntly: the territory is spending more than it can afford, leaving it in a “dangerous financial position.”

items.title: N.W.T. residents who heat home with oil may pay more due to war in Middle East
items.summary_auto: A seniors' advocate in Yellowknife says she is concerned that the global jump in oil prices could push seniors into poverty.

items.title: Immigration rules hamper health-care recruitment, nurse says after permanent residency application rejected
items.summary_auto: A nurse in Winnipeg says Canada's immigration system is undercutting efforts to address a shortage of health-care workers, after his application for permanent residency was rejected on a technicality.

items.title: Iran will not play World Cup games in U.S., says soccer chief
items.summary_auto: Iran were one of the first nations to qualify for the finals but their participation has been in doubt since the conflict between the Islamic Republic and the United States began in late February.

items.title: Tehran intensifies attacks on Gulf energy facilities after Israel hits Iranian gas field
items.summary_auto: Iran intensified its attacks on its Gulf Arab neighbours' energy infrastructure Thursday, hitting a Saudi refinery on the Red Sea and setting Qatari liquefied natural gas facilities and two Kuwaiti oil refineries ablaze as it struck back following an Israeli attack on its main natural gas field.

items.title: N.L. government expropriated this family's home 14 years ago for a highway. It sold the land instead
items.summary_auto: Hannah and Cyndie Stapleton said they were gobsmacked to discover a new home is being built where their former home once stood — over 13 years after it was expropriated. Now, Transportation and Infrastructure Minister Barry Petten says the department is developing a new land disposition policy.

items.title: Sharp increase in rehiring of retired nurses in N.L. underscores recruitment and retention problems: union
items.summary_auto: Data obtained by CBC News shows six retired nurses were working in N.L. a decade ago. That number has jumped to 253.

items.title: Are we in for a super El Niño this year? Early projections say maybe, and this worries climate scientists
items.summary_auto: After a year and a half of La Niña conditions, it looks like we could end up with an El Niño later this year. And early models suggest it could be a strong one, which could push global temperatures to record highs.

items.title: 2 Canadian girls stuck in Egypt under a travel ban requested by father in Canada
items.summary_auto: Two Canadian girls are currently stuck in Egypt due to a travel ban obtained by their Egyptian-Canadian father who is living in Canada. The children's mother is trying to get the ban lifted so she can return home to Canada with her daughters.

items.title: Here are Canada's biggest points of leverage in tariff and trade talks with the U.S.
items.summary_auto: While Canada's economy is far more reliant on exports to the U.S. than vice versa, Canadian negotiators have crucial ammunition in their efforts to land a trade deal that reduces or eliminates tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: Fortnite is hiking cost of its currency to 'pay the bills.' Are its battles against Google and Apple to blame?
items.summary_auto: The popular online gaming platform Fortnite is hiking the price of its in-game currency starting Thursday, a rare move that experts say sheds some light on the cost of its parent company's antitrust battles against tech giants Google and Apple.

items.title: Israel keeps eliminating Iran's top leaders. How is it impacting the war?
items.summary_auto: Iran continues to launch missiles, raising questions as to whether its mosaic defence doctrine has enabled the country to fight on despite the decapitation of its top leadership.
